EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe weather outbreak occurred during the mid to late afternoon hours of Thursday, June 19th, 2025, as a strong cold front supported by a seasonably strong upper-level jet intersected a hot, humid, and unstable airmass. Severe weather was exclusively in the form of damaging wind gusts, many of which were considerable (near hurricane-force) in nature in the Washington DC metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 62 knots (71 MPH) was measured by a personal weather station at Great Falls Crossing. Dozens of trees blew down in the area, with many onto wires and a few onto houses. (LWX)

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms developed over western Maryland, then gathered strength as it moved east. Destructive winds, very large hail, and a tornado were observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Half dollar size hail was reported in Bailey's Crossroads. (LWX)

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front. Several became severe, producing wind damage and giant hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter size hail was reported in Herndon. (LWX)

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Behind a potent cold front, strong gradient winds developed across much of the region during the late morning to afternoon hours on February 16.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at Washington Dulles International Airport, KIAD, measured a peak wind gust of 62 mph at 1539LST. (LWX)

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe weather outbreak occurred during the mid to late afternoon hours of Thursday, June 19th, 2025.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees blew down on I-95 south of VA-630 Courthouse Road. (LWX)

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms produced destructive winds, very large hail, and a tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brief EF1 tornado touched down near Franconia, snapping trees and damaging roofs. (LWX)
